Diabetes.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes among adults.
Diabetes.long_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes among adults, from BRFSS (self-reported diagnosis), Epic Cosmos (HbA1c ≥6.5% or ICD-10 diagnosis codes), and Medicare FFS (CCW algorithm). The source column identifies the data source and clinical definition used.

Obesity.short_description: Estimated prevalence of obesity (BMI ≥30) among adults.
Obesity.long_description: Estimated prevalence of obesity among adults, from BRFSS (self-reported height/weight, BMI ≥30), Epic Cosmos (measured BMI ≥30 or ICD-10 diagnosis codes), and Medicare FFS (CCW algorithm). The source column identifies the data source and clinical definition used.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/brfss_preva.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity from BRFSS telephone survey, by state and year.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/brfss_preva.long_description: Age-stratified state-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from the CDC Behavioral Risk Factor Surveillance System (BRFSS). Diabetes prevalence is based on self-reported ever-diagnosis; obesity is based on calculated BMI ≥30 from self-reported height and weight. The outcome_name column identifies the condition. Includes 95% confidence intervals (value_lcl, value_ucl) and survey sample size.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/brfss_preva.long_name: Lower 95% Confidence Interval for BRFSS Prevalence
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/brfss_preva.short_description: Lower bound of the 95% confidence interval for BRFSS chronic disease prevalence.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/brfss_preva.long_name: Upper 95% Confidence Interval for BRFSS Prevalence
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/brfss_preva.short_description: Upper bound of the 95% confidence interval for BRFSS chronic disease prevalence.
Number of BRFSS survey respondents used to estimate chronic disease prevalence.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_name: Epic Cosmos State-Level Chronic Disease Prevalence
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity from Epic Cosmos EHR, by state and year.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_description: State-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from Epic Cosmos electronic health records. The source column identifies the clinical definition: 'Epic Cosmos: HbA1c' uses HbA1c ≥6.5% for diabetes; 'Epic Cosmos: ICD10' uses CCW ICD-10 diagnosis codes for both conditions; 'Epic Cosmos: BMI' uses measured BMI ≥30 for obesity. Values represent the percentage of active Epic patients meeting the specified criterion.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_name: Percentage of 2021 State Population Captured in Epic Cosmos
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.short_description: Percentage of the 2021 state population represented in the Epic Cosmos patient panel, by age group.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_description: Estimated percentage of the 2021 Census population in each age group that is represented in the Epic Cosmos patient panel for a given state. Used as an indicator of Epic's geographic coverage and potential selection bias. Lower values indicate that Epic data covers a smaller fraction of the state's population.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_name: Number of Epic Cosmos Patients in State-Level Analysis
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.short_description: Number of Epic Cosmos patients in the state-level analysis. Values ≤10 are reported as '10 or fewer'.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_name: County-Level Chronic Disease Prevalence from Epic and Medicare
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity at county level from Epic Cosmos or Medicare FFS.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_description: County-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from Epic Cosmos (2025 snapshot) and Medicare Fee-for-Service (CMS, most recent year). For Epic Cosmos: 'Epic Cosmos: HbA1c' uses HbA1c ≥6.5% for diabetes; 'Epic Cosmos: BMI' uses measured BMI ≥30 for obesity. For Medicare FFS: diagnosis based on CCW ICD-10 algorithm. The source column identifies the data source and clinical definition.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_name: Percentage of 2021 County Population Captured in Epic Cosmos
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.short_description: Percentage of the 2021 county population represented in the Epic Cosmos patient panel, by age group.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.long_name: Number of Patients in County-Level Analysis
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/epic_preval.short_description: Number of patients used in the county-level analysis. Small counts from Epic Cosmos are reported as '10 or fewer'.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.long_name: Chronic Disease Prevalence by Geography, Year, and Source
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.short_description: Estimated prevalence of diabetes or obesity by state and year from multiple sources.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.long_description: Combined state-level prevalence estimates for diabetes and obesity from CDC BRFSS (telephone survey), Epic Cosmos EHR (HbA1c, ICD-10, and BMI-based definitions), and Medicare Fee-for-Service administrative claims. The outcome_name column identifies the condition (Diabetes or Obesity); the source column identifies the data source and clinical definition. These sources are not directly comparable due to differences in population covered (all adults vs. Epic patients vs. Medicare beneficiaries ≥65), case definitions, and ascertainment methodology.

bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.short_description: Survey respondents (BRFSS), patient count (Epic), or beneficiary count (Medicare) for the prevalence estimate.
bundle_chronic_diseases/dist/prevalence_.long_description: Sample size underlying the prevalence estimate. Interpretation varies by source: for BRFSS, this is the number of survey respondents; for Epic Cosmos, this is the number of active patients (values ≤10 are reported as '10 or fewer'); for Medicare FFS, this is the number of beneficiaries with a Medicare claim.
